content: {"analyses": [{"source_url": "https://www.semanticscholar.org/paper/c2c30517d0c25f0a4961c94a34587bb119e12ca7", "source_title": "Physical characterization and bond performance of a non-methacrylate dental adhesive in long-term biochemical and thermal aging models.", "source_type": "journal_article", "source_quality": 0.68, "overall_relevance": 0.22, "year": 0, "authors": [], "venue": "Dental Materials", "doi": "", "atomic_facts": [{"statement": "Biochemical aging of the commercial methacrylate adhesive in simulated human salivary esterase media led to a significant 20% decrease in interfacial fracture toughness and 33% decrease in stiffness after only 6 months, while the experimental biostable adhesive experienced no changes in bonding performance following the entire 1-year duration.", "direct_quote": "biochemical aging of the C-MA in SHSE led to a significant decrease (p < 0.05) in KIC (20 %) and stiffness (33 %) following just 6 months of aging. In contrast, the EA 5 experienced no changes (p > 0.05) in bonding performance following the entire duration (1 year) of biochemical aging. While thermal aging had no effect (p > 0.05) on the interfacial mechanics and fracture mode of the EA 5 or C-MA", "fact_category": "measurement", "relevance_score": 0.25, "confidence": 0.85, "perspective": "Scientific", "entities": ["interfacial fracture toughness", "KIC", "stiffness", "biochemical aging", "simulated human salivary esterase", "methacrylate adhesive"]}, {"statement": "Nine candidate experimental adhesives were screened for physical properties including degree of conversion, surface microhardness, surface water contact angle, and microtensile bond strength, then subjected to thermocycling at 10,000 cycles between 5-50 degrees Celsius or 1 year of biochemical aging in simulated human salivary esterase media.", "direct_quote": "9 candidate EAs were screened for their as-prepared physical properties (degree of conversion, surface microhardness, surface water contact angle) and microtensile bond strength (µTBS) and compared to the properties of both the C-MA and IH-MA.
